sections.label: ASSISTANT
sections.content: World model: Current state matches W=blue, g=orange target but level incomplete. Need to try clicking center cells or reconsider target. Let me try clicking center cells first.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Try clicking center of C1 (the orange core at rows 24-25, cols 22-23)
# Based on ascii, C1 is at rows 22-27, the core O is at rows 24-25
# Looking at the ascii line: ccccccccccccOOOOOOccggOOggccOOOOOOccggbbggccOOOOOOcccccccccc
# C1 core starts after "ccgg" which is 4 chars after "ccOOOOOOcc" which is 12 chars
# Let me count: cccccccccccc (12) OOOOOO (6) cc (2) gg (2) OO (2) gg (2) cc (2)
# So C1 core OO is at cols 22-23 (0-indexed from the start of the line)
# But the ascii is scaled. Let me use the segmentation to find exact positions.

seg = current_frame.segmentation
nodes = seg['nodes']

# Find nodes that are part of center cells (small colored regions inside the 6x6 blocks)
# The center cells have cores with colors O or b
small_nodes = []
for n in nodes:
    if n['pixels'] < 36 and n['pixels'] >= 4:
        b = n['boundary']
        r_min = min(p[0] for p in b)
        c_min = min(p[1] for p in b)
        small_nodes.append((n['id'], n['color'], n['pixels'], (r_min, c_min)))

print("Small nodes (potential center cell parts):")
for sn in sorted(small_nodes, key=lambda x: (x[3][0], x[3][1])):
    print(f"  id={sn[0]} color={sn[1]} pixels={sn[2]} pos={sn[3]}")

# Try clicking center of C1 core (around row 24, col 23)
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 24, 'col': 23}])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
